sections.heading: Annual Data Privacy Training
body: This training is required for all employees and contractors with access to sensitive data.

sections.heading: Module 1: What Is Data Privacy
body: Data privacy refers to the rules governing how we collect, use, store, and share information about individuals.

Federal and state laws set strict requirements for handling Personally Identifiable Information (PII), Federal Tax Information (FTI), Protected Health Information (PHI), and Social Security Administration (SSA) data.

sections.heading: Why It Matters
body: Each record we hold represents a real person who trusted us with sensitive information. When data is mishandled:
bullets: Individuals can be harmed by identity theft, fraud, and exposure of personal information.
The agency faces regulatory fines, civil settlements, and loss of public trust.
Federal agencies may suspend or terminate the agency's access to FTI.
Individual employees may face personal civil and criminal liability.

sections.heading: Module 2: Handling Sensitive Data
body: Five categories of data are treated as sensitive at our agency. Each has specific rules but they share common principles.

sections.heading: Need to Know
body: Access is granted based on job function, not seniority or personal interest. Even with general clearance, employees should access only the specific records required for their assigned tasks.

sections.heading: Secure Transmission
body: Sensitive data must never be sent through unapproved channels. This includes:

sections.heading: Module 3: Reporting Incidents
body: If you suspect any unauthorized access, disclosure, or loss of sensitive data, report it immediately. The 24-hour reporting clock starts when you suspect a problem, not when you have all the details.
False alarms are fine. Silence is not.
